title: ferray-ma: no fill_value concept — masked elements get T::zero() in operations
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's MaskedArray has a fill_value attribute that's used as the replacement value for masked elements during operations. Different dtypes have different default fill_values (1e20 for float, 999999 for int, etc.). ferray-ma hardcodes T::zero() for masked elements in ufunc_support.rs: 'if *m { T::zero() } else { f(*v) }'. This means: (1) no customizable fill, (2) operations like log/sqrt on masked data may produce 0 in masked positions instead of a sentinel, (3) filled() must be called with an explicit value. Add a fill_value: T field to MaskedArray and use it as the default for filled() and operation outputs.
